Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

I have to agree with Andreas. Also I had this patch in my test matrix and I just tracked it down to this patch why sanity 133g would hang my test bed system.

Patch Set 6:

Andreas, removing procfs entries from Lustre and replacing them by sysfs -- is it still a goal ?

Patch Set 6:

Cannot build patch due to 14, messages:

 make[1]: *** No rule to make target '../ldiskfs/kernel_patches/series/ldiskfs-', needed by 'sources'.
autoMakefile:1022: recipe for target 'ldiskfs-sources' failed
make: *** [ldiskfs-sources] Error 2

Patch Set 6:

Yes, the upstream client has already moved all /proc entries over to /sys and lctl and lfs have been updated since 2.8.0 to check for tunables in both locations. At some point soon, maybe 2.10, we will do the same with the master client and server as well. 

As I write previously, it probably doesn't make sense to expose this directly to users. Instead, a fail_loc can be used for this.

parents.subject: LU-10650 obd: add check to obd_statfs

commit.subject: LU-5695 libcfs: watchdog dispatch thread fix
commit.message:
LU-5695 libcfs: watchdog dispatch thread fix

lc_watchdogd may stop imediately after start
because nobody clears the stop flag.

Xyratex-bug-id: MRP-2108 MRP-1913
Change-Id: I1eaaf0330c111b7f2b17081c716ef8c200677d6b
Signed-off-by: Alexander Zarochentsev <alexander.zarochentsev@seagate.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/12155
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
